content.problemStatement:
The MEX of a set of integers is defined as the smallest non-negative integer that does not belong to this set. For example, MEX({0,2,3})=1\mathrm{MEX}(\{0,2,3\}) = 1MEX({0,2,3})=1 and MEX({1,3})=0\mathrm{MEX}(\{1,3\}) = 0MEX({1,3})=0.

Chef has a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). The tree is rooted at node 111. Chef wants to assign a non-negative integer to each node in such a way that each integer between 000 and N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 (inclusive) is assigned to exactly one node.

For each node uuu, consider the integers assigned to the nodes in the subtree of uuu (including uuu); let aua_uau‚Äã denote the MEX of these integers. Chef wants a1+a2+‚Ä¶+aNa_1 + a_2 + \ldots + a_Na1‚Äã+a2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+aN‚Äã to be as large as possible. Find the maximum possible value of this sum.
constraints: 1‚â§pi<i1 \le p_i \lt i1‚â§pi‚Äã<i for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pN‚àí1p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{N-1}p1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚àí1‚Äã. For each valid iii, the node pip_ipi‚Äã is the parent of the node i+1i+1i+1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the maximum sum of subtree MEX-s which can be obtained if you assign the weights optimally.
